events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Need parse scenario. Heavy industrial, electrical equipment bonding. Observed: CSST gas line not bonded. Work rough-complete. Need identify component/condition and visible defect cues. Decision fail because non-compliant/dangerous? CSST gas line not bonded is a defect; risk maybe high due to arcing/puncture/fire/explosion. Component conditions installed-defective, non-compliant. percent_complete rough-complete maybe 70? value not asked 0. sound_source etc default 0/empty. Findings: CSST gas line lacks bonding jumper/connection to grounding electrode system; no visible bonding conductor or clamp; potential for electrical arcing/puncture/fire. Actions: install listed bonding jumper per NEC 250.104(B) or manufacturer; bond to service equipment/grounding electrode; inspect/test continuity. Rationale: NEC requires gas piping (CSST) to be bonded; unbonded CSST is shock/fire hazard. References NEC 250.104(B), 250.30 maybe separately grounded systems. Need ensure JSON valid.

    "CSST gas line is installed but has no visible bonding jumper or bonding clamp",
    "No connection from the CSST to the grounding electrode system or service equipment enclosure",
    "Unbonded CSST can be energized by fault current, risking arc damage, puncture, fire, or explosion"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Install a listed bonding jumper sized per NEC 250.104(B) and manufacturer requirements",
    "Bond the CSST gas piping to the service equipment enclosure, grounded conductor, grounding electrode conductor, or grounding electrode",
    "Verify continuity and inspect before closing out or energizing"
  ],
  "rationale": "NEC 250.104 requires gas piping, including CSST, to be bonded to the grounding electrode system. An unbonded CSST line at the rough-complete stage is a non-compliant, installed-defective condition that creates a shock and fire hazard, so the installation fails.",
  "references": ["NEC 250.104", "NEC 250.30"]
}
